Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.view.ships

Class Ship3D

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.Group
com.example.battleship.view.ships.Ship3D
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
Direct Known Subclasses:
AircraftCarrier3D, Destroyer3D, Frigate3D, Submarine3D
public abstract class Ship3D extends javafx.scene.Group
3D representation of a fleet ship, built from JavaFX Shape3D subclasses (Box, Cylinder, Sphere).

Applies the Template Method pattern: this class handles everything common to any ship (positioning on the board, orientation, per-cell hit tracking), while each concrete subclass (Frigate3D, Destroyer3D, Submarine3D, AircraftCarrier3D) only needs to implement buildHull() to define its distinctive look.

Important: the hull is built as a single continuous piece of geometry spanning the whole length of the ship, not as one disconnected chunk per occupied cell -- otherwise the ship reads visually as a row of separate blocks instead of one vessel. Grid cells only matter for hit-tracking logic and for where the per-cell hit marker is placed; they never split the hull itself. When the whole ship is sunk, the entire hull is recolored at once, so the finished "wreck" always shows the full, uninterrupted ship design.

  • Field Details

    • sizeInCells

      private final int anchorRow

    • anchorColumn

      private final int anchorColumn

    • orientation

      private final Orientation orientation

      private final List<SegmentState> states

      private final List<javafx.scene.Group> hitMarks

  • Constructor Details

    • Ship3D

      protected Ship3D(int sizeInCells, int anchorRow, int anchorColumn, Orientation orientation)
      Parameters:
      sizeInCells - number of cells occupied by the ship (1 to 4)
      anchorRow - board row where the ship starts (0-9)
      anchorColumn - board column where the ship starts (0-9)
      orientation - HORIZONTAL (along columns) or VERTICAL (along rows)

  • Method Details

    • buildHull

      protected abstract javafx.scene.Group buildHull()
      Builds the ship's entire hull as one continuous group of Shape3D nodes (Box, Cylinder, Sphere), spanning its full length. Use cellCenterX(int) to place distinctive features (a bridge, a periscope, an island...) at a specific cell, and shipCenterX() to center pieces that span the whole ship.
      Returns:
      group representing the complete hull

    • cellCenterX

      protected final double cellCenterX(int index)
      X offset of the center of cell index within this ship's local coordinates.

    • shipCenterX

      protected final double shipCenterX()
      X offset of the midpoint of the whole ship, useful to center a full-length hull piece.

    • placeOnBoard

      private void placeOnBoard()
      Positions and (for a vertical ship) rotates this whole node to sit at its anchor cell, facing the right way.

    • markHit

      public void markHit(int segmentIndex)
      Marks one cell of the ship as hit. This does not alter the hull geometry or color -- it only scatters a wreckage cluster (broken fragments, fire, smoke -- see WreckageDebris3D) over the affected cell, the same for every ship type. If every cell ends up hit, the whole ship transitions to the sunk state.
      Parameters:
      segmentIndex - index of the hit cell (0-based)

    • markSunk

      private void markSunk()
      Sinks the ship: removes the individual per-cell hit markers and recolors the whole hull in a single pass, so the result is always the complete ship silhouette in the "sunk" material -- never a grid-chopped patchwork. Also always reveals the hull, even if it was hidden (see setHullVisible(boolean)) -- once a ship is fully destroyed, the opponent gets to see the whole wreck as confirmation, whether or not the "cheat" reveal (HU-3) is active.

    • setHullVisible

      public void setHullVisible(boolean visible)
      Shows or hides just this ship's hull -- not the whole node, so hit markers (markHit(int)) stay visible even while the hull itself is hidden. Used for HU-3: the machine's fleet starts hidden and can be revealed (all at once, via the "cheat" code) or per-ship once it's sunk (see markSunk()).
      Parameters:
      visible - true to show the hull, false to hide it

    • markAsPreview

      public void markAsPreview()
      Recolors the whole hull to a flat, semi-bright "ghost" material, for showing this ship as a placement preview.

      Deliberately does NOT use setOpacity(): real transparency on a compound 3D Group with many overlapping meshes, inside a depth-buffered SubScene, renders unreliably -- in practice the whole node can simply fail to appear. Swapping every material to one flat color, the same technique already used by markSunk(), stays fully opaque and renders exactly as reliably as any other ship state.

    • markAsInvalidPreview

      public void markAsInvalidPreview()
      Recolors the whole hull red, for showing this ship as a preview that can't actually be placed here.

    • applyMaterialRecursively

      private void applyMaterialRecursively(javafx.scene.Node node, javafx.scene.paint.PhongMaterial material)
      Recursively applies a material to every Shape3D found under node.

    • isFullyHit

      private boolean isFullyHit()
      Returns:
      true once every segment is at least HIT (hit or already sunk) -- the trigger for markSunk()

    • validateIndex

      private void validateIndex(int index)
      Parameters:
      index - a segment index to check
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index isn't a valid segment of this ship

    • isSunk

      public boolean isSunk()
      Returns:
      true once every segment of this ship has been sunk

    • getSizeInCells

      public int getSizeInCells()
      Returns:
      how many cells this ship occupies

    • getAnchorRow

      public int getAnchorRow()
      Returns:
      the board row of this ship's anchor cell (its bow)

    • getAnchorColumn

      public int getAnchorColumn()
      Returns:
      the board column of this ship's anchor cell (its bow)

    • getOrientation

      public Orientation getOrientation()
      Returns:
      the direction this ship's remaining segments extend in, from its anchor cell

    • getState

      public SegmentState getState(int segmentIndex)
      Parameters:
      segmentIndex - a segment index (0-based)
      Returns:
      that segment's current visual state (intact, hit, or sunk)
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if segmentIndex isn't a valid segment of this ship
